problem: Focused audio cue sweeps can find local candidates that look promising in isolation, then fail once placed in the full Aurora theme and event-gap scoring context.
strategy: Replace one cue in the latest full-theme metrics with a representative candidate capture, run the normal event-gap scorer against that synthetic theme, and block runtime promotion unless the cue and aggregate guardrails improve.
successMeasure: A candidate may be promoted only when focused keeper gates, full-theme cue gap, worst-segment risk, theme average risk, and semantic score all hold or improve.

candidate.keeperRead: segment risk improved only -0.06; loss composite role gate failed; loss composite score 6.56 < 7.2

blockers: No Ship Loss Body candidate cleared whole-cue, segment, duration, band, centroid, and role-match gates.

currentCueRow.label: Ship Loss
currentCueRow.focus: Compare whether ship loss feels like a real Galaga death moment rather than a generic impact.
currentCueRow.worstSegmentInterpretation: tail timbre is the leading gap: band shape/rolloff differ from the reference segment.
currentCueRow.referenceSegmentationResolution: relative to full galaga3-death clip; labels preserve long death onset, sustained body, and final tail
currentCueRow.referenceSegmentationSource: curated from active-window reads in aurora-audio-theme-comparison and repeated playerHit candidate pass
currentCueRow.playerMeaning: The player lost a ship; the cue should be unmistakable and synchronized with death feedback.
currentCueRow.semanticRecommendation: Keep semantic mapping as a guardrail while acoustic/timing gaps are tuned.
currentCueRow.recommendation: Tune the tail segment first: tail timbre is the leading gap: band shape/rolloff differ from the reference segment.

simulatedCueRow.label: Ship Loss (promotion precheck)
simulatedCueRow.focus: Compare whether ship loss feels like a real Galaga death moment rather than a generic impact.
simulatedCueRow.worstSegmentInterpretation: body timing/duration differs enough to tune event pacing before timbre.
simulatedCueRow.referenceSegmentationResolution: relative to full galaga3-death clip; labels preserve long death onset, sustained body, and final tail
simulatedCueRow.referenceSegmentationSource: curated from active-window reads in aurora-audio-theme-comparison and repeated playerHit candidate pass
simulatedCueRow.playerMeaning: The player lost a ship; the cue should be unmistakable and synchronized with death feedback.
simulatedCueRow.semanticRecommendation: Keep semantic mapping as a guardrail while acoustic/timing gaps are tuned.
simulatedCueRow.recommendation: Tune the body segment first: body timing/duration differs enough to tune event pacing before timbre.

nextStep: Do not promote playerHit; use this precheck evidence to pivot toward a stronger candidate family or the current highest-risk cue (playerHit).
